Math & Science · Length Conversions

Millimeters to Inches Converter

Convert millimeters to inches with decimal and fractional output plus significant figures.

visual
0
Fractional: –
Visual ruler

Fractions map to common 1/16 inch marks; metric ticks show exact spacing.

Inputs & variables

  • Value
  • Direction (mm ↔ in)
  • Significant figures
  • Fractional rounding (nearest 1/16)

Theory

Exact factor: 1 inch = 25.4 mm (standardized in 1959 by international agreement).

Formula: inches = millimeters / 25.4; reverse: millimeters = inches × 25.4.

Use significant figures to reflect measurement uncertainty.

Examples

  • 50 mm → 1.9685 in (≈ 1 31/32) for 3D printing tolerances.
  • 200 mm → 7.8740 in for mechanical parts or displays.

FAQ

Why 25.4 is exact?

The international inch was fixed at 25.4 mm in 1959; it is not an approximation.

Can I see fractional inches?

Yes, output includes the nearest 1/16 inch and the decimal value.

How do significant figures work?

Pick sig figs to match your input precision; results round accordingly.

Export results, compare scenarios, and get pro guidance.
